g.text: Kernel Module Global State (read by mydevice_proc_show)

ul.li: buf is limited to ~4096 bytes → silent truncation

Thousands of kernel drivers got it wrong historically seq_file solves this: your show() callback writes to an expandable internal buffer via seq_printf(). seq_read() handles all fragmentation automatically.

text.tspan: NOTE: do NOT check seq_printf return value
EOF flag set for next read()
1. Check if seq_file internal buffer populated
2. If empty: call show() to generate content
5. If all data delivered: return 0 (EOF signal)
automatically paged across multiple read() calls
Modified by: read / write / CLEAR / RESIZE
